texto: It is also interesting to observe that communal ownership violates every instinct of human nature. It destroys initiative, nullifies free agency, suppresses inventive exploration, minimizes the dignity of the individual and makes a god out of an abstract thing called "The State"- to which is delegated complete, unrestricted control over life, liberty and property. . . Like so many other weak systems of government, it can survive only in an atmosphere of a slave state, ruled by a king or a dictator.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a W. Cleon Skousen: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “It is also interesting to observe that communal ownership violates every instinct of human nature…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, freedom, liberty pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de liberty e private-property.
